notes: Behavioral equivalence verified: authorization logic (student/lecturer/admin checks, error cases) matches pre-refactor endpoint code from commit afc855f

Exception taxonomy is sound: SubmissionNotFoundException (404 empty), SubmissionForbiddenException (403), SubmissionValidationException (400), SubmissionAssignmentNotFoundException (404 with body), SubmissionAttempt{Limit,Conflict}Exception (409) all distinct and correctly mapped at endpoint boundary
Stream ownership correctly respected: Service.UploadAsync only reads streams without disposing; endpoint maintains ownership via await using blocks

Transaction boundaries preserved: Service does not open transactions; Serializable transaction in CreateWithAttemptCheckAsync stays within Repository layer; external calls (catalog, storage, eventBus) happen after transaction commits
Dependency boundaries enforced: Service layer contains no Microsoft.AspNetCore types (ClaimsPrincipal, IFormFile, IResult); requires plain RequesterContext and UploadSubmissionCommand records

Authorization checks correctly refactored: EnsureCanActOnAsync extracted as private helper for GetForRequesterAsync and RetryAsync, reusing GetLecturerAllowedStudentIdsAsync for consistent lecturer enrollment filtering across all operations

Endpoint validation moved correctly: TryBuildRequesterContext validates NameIdentifier claim → Guid for students before service call (matching old inline checks at lines 32, 61, 82, 112, 133 from pre-refactor code)

Attempt exception handling: SubmissionAttempt{Limit,Conflict}Exception from repository propagate unchanged to endpoint (allowed by phase spec 'or let them propagate') with correct status code (409 Conflict) and response body
Idempotency note correctly recorded: retry endpoint pre-existing lack of de-duplication (calling twice enqueues job twice) preserved as-is per behavior-preservation scope; documented in service code
dotnet build: 0 errors, 0 warnings (phase verification prerequisite met)
